title: Dialectic Engine

fields.vision: Dialectic Engine is a MuranoOS cognitive-engine-room handoff for preserving narrative, philosophical, and argument-state relations.
fields.mechanism: It defines a relation grammar, synchronized view topology, interaction model, UI/UX path, MVP implementation phasing, and MuranoOS protocol so a story, chapter, or argument can be inspected through tensions, positions, transformations, and continuity rather than flattened into notes.
fields.source_utilization: Political theory, democracy, individuality, and civic formation. Use source library political theory as a central source reservoir for the Book Trilogy and democratic-formation pages. Vision: Give Book II, democracy, and civic-formation pages a political-theory spine rather than generic civics language. Mechanism: Extract liberty, equality, tyranny, statesmanship, party government, normativity, democracy, and Machiavellian institutional mechanisms. Plan: Close-read Book Trilogy and political-theory pages before importing formulations. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need exact passages before public quotations or stronger scholarly claims. Source pool: 76 deduplicated local title candidates; 80 catalogue support rows. Examples: On Liberalism; Democracy and Its Fragility. Discovery, creativity, systems thinking, and project generation. Use source library creativity/innovation/network sources to professionalize the project-development account. Vision: Explain Nexus and Personal OS as project-development and discovery infrastructure, not miscellaneous personal tooling. Mechanism: Extract creativity, network, learning, imagination, leadership, and project-generation mechanisms. Plan: Rewrite Nexus and planning pages around upstream/downstream project movement. Status: source library identified as candidate scholarly support; direct quotation requires exact passage review. Remaining gap: Need exact project-method and creativity passages. Source pool: 151 deduplicated local title candidates; 154 catalogue support rows. Examples: Creativity Research; The Heritage, Creativity and Innovation Nexus. source library scholarly-source routing with author correction. Raw filenames often append Nik_Murano and are not treated as authorship evidence. Catalogue rows are...
fields.plan: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. When to reach for this: You have a substantial story/philosophical work (novel, screenplay, mythopoetic project) and want to design a MuranoOS room that preserves narrative+symbolic+ethical+cinematic layers as linked synchronized views rather than flat notes. Use only as design input to Pass 2 planning — do not start implementation from Pass 1. Entry point: 00_STATUS_MANIFEST.md (to confirm NOT DONE) — 01_ROOM_OVERVIEW.md — 02_INTERFACE_BLUEPRINT.md — 07_MURANOOS_PROTOCOL_ADDENDUM.md. Integration target: MuranoOS desktop app, but blocked — requires MuranoOS source docs or architecture answers before Pass 2. Wire or preserve the implementation files: None. Pass 1 is pure design. Implementation scaffold is the Pass 4 deliverable under Track B and would include component/file structure, typed schemas, store contracts, command stubs.
fields.remaining_gaps: Promote the packet from documented handoff to live implementation only after the declared caveats are resolved: apply or wire the scaffold, run the stated validation or smoke-test sequence, and keep unresolved assumptions visible.
fields.detailed_source_vision: The Dialectic Engine Room is a "lossless narrative-philosophical cognition chamber" for designing, inspecting, revising, and preserving a story system whose atomic units are simultaneously narrative, structural, symbolic, ethical, cinematic, and recursive. It is explicitly classified as a Cognitive Engine Room — not an operational app room — and the organizing principle is the Truth-Freedom-Authenticity (TFAT) triad mapped spatially onto the interface. This is Pass 1 of a 3-to-4-pass sequence and is explicitly NOT DONE. The pack converts vision into a concrete handoff foundation: room identity, UI/UX blueprint, map + relation grammar, synchronized view topology, interaction model, MVP implementation phasing, and a MuranoOS protocol addendum for cognitive-engine rooms. It cannot advance to Pass 2 without supplied MuranoOS architectural facts (runtime, room inventory, shared component library, persistence choice, router/manifest format...
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: , state the backend/frontend or packet components, and decide whether the handoff is ready for engineering, public explanation, or archival retention

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: Dialectic Engine is a MuranoOS cognitive-engine-room handoff for preserving narrative, philosophical, and argument-state relations.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It defines a relation grammar, synchronized view topology, interaction model, UI/UX path, MVP implementation phasing, and MuranoOS protocol so a story, chapter, or argument can be inspected through tensions, positions, transformations, and continuity rather than flattened into notes.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It matters because serious writing needs a room that preserves the structure of thought as it changes, not just a document editor.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It matters because serious writing needs a room that preserves the structure of thought as it changes, not just a document editor.

instruments.downstream_use: How this briefing says the vision should be accomplished, translated into a public project route. When to reach for this: You have a substantial story/philosophical work (novel, screenplay, mythopoetic project) and want to design a MuranoOS room that.
instruments.proof: This is Pass 1 of a 3-to-4-pass sequence and is explicitly NOT DONE. The pack converts vision into a concrete handoff foundation: room identity, UI/UX blueprint, map + relation grammar, synchronized view topology, interaction model, MVP implementation.
instruments.gap: Promote the packet from documented handoff to live implementation only after the declared caveats are resolved: apply or wire the scaffold, run the stated validation or smoke-test sequence, and keep unresolved.
instruments.verification_check: Confirm `NOT DONE` appears in the cited source context and that the public page explains what output, decision, or project function it supports.
